use std::sync::OnceLock;

struct ConnectionPool {
    connections: Vec<String>,
}

fn create_pool() -> ConnectionPool {
    std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5));
    ConnectionPool {
        connections: (0..100).map(|i| format!("conn_{}", i)).collect(),
    }
}

static POOL: OnceLock<ConnectionPool> = OnceLock::new();

// GOOD: explicit warmup during startup, before serving traffic
async fn startup() {
    tokio::task::spawn_blocking(|| {
        POOL.get_or_init(|| create_pool());
    }).await.unwrap();
}

async fn handle_request() -> &'static ConnectionPool {
    POOL.get().expect("startup must complete first")
}
